info.title: Zenduty
info.description:
# Introduction

Zenduty is a cutting edge platform for incident management. With high level automation, Zenduty enables faster and better incident resolution keeping developers first.


  Javascript Libraries: https://github.com/Zenduty/zenduty-js

  Ruby Libraries: https://github.com/Zenduty/zenduty-ruby

  Python Libraries: https://github.com/Zenduty/zenduty-python-sdk

  Postman Collection for Zenduty API: https://github.com/Zenduty/zenduty-api-postman.git

  Account-level roles and permissions: https://docs.zenduty.com/docs/users#account-level-roles-and-permissions

  Team-level roles and permissions: https://docs.zenduty.com/docs/users#team-level-roles-and-permissions

tags.name: Account Member
tags.description: This object represents a user of the account. Each account member object has a role, which can be owner, admin, or user. Each account has a single owner, but the admins and users can be multiple.

tags.name: Account Custom Role
tags.description: This object represents a custom role of the account. Each custom role object has custom permissions, which the account admin can set from account level permissions and assign this custom role to a particular user.

tags.name: User Custom Role
tags.description: This object represents a user's custom role. Each user can be assigned to only one custom role.

tags.name: Notification Rules
tags.description: This object represents a user's notification rules. A user can have notification rules for high urgency and low urgency incidents.

tags.name: Forwarding Rules
tags.description: This object represents a user's forwarding rules. A user can forward his notifications to the other account members in case an incident occurs.

tags.name: Contact Methods
tags.description: This object represents one of the following communication channels(Email, SMS, Phone Call, Slack, Microsoft Team, Google Chat). A user can add multiple contacts methods.

tags.name: Teams
tags.description: This object represents a team of the account. It lets you create different independent operational units in the account. You can check out the team docs here https://docs.zenduty.com/docs/teams.

tags.name: Team Members
tags.description: This object represents the users of a team. Each team member has a role which can be a manager or a user. You can checkout the team member docs here https://docs.zenduty.com/docs/users

tags.name: Team Permissions
tags.description: This object represents the permissions of a team. It lets you access the particular team elements that the team has given permissions to use.

tags.name: Schedules
tags.description: This object represents the schedule of a team. You can checkout the schedule docs here https://docs.zenduty.com/docs/schedules

tags.name: Schedule Overrides
tags.description: This object represents the Override of a Schedule. You can checkout the Schedule Override docs here https://docs.zenduty.com/docs/schedules/#schedule-override

tags.name: Escalation Policies
tags.description: This object represents the escalation policy of a team. You can checkout the escalation policy docs here https://docs.zenduty.com/docs/escalationpolicies

tags.name: Services
tags.description: This object represents the services associated with a team. You can checkout the services docs here https://docs.zenduty.com/docs/services

tags.name: Integrations
tags.description: This object represents the integrations associated with a service. You can checkout the integration docs here https://docs.zenduty.com/docs/integrations

tags.name: Incidents
tags.description: This object represents the incidents of the account. You can checkout the incident docs here https://docs.zenduty.com/docs/incidents

tags.name: Incident Roles
tags.description: This object represents the incident roles of an incident. You can checkout the incident role docs here https://docs.zenduty.com/docs/incidentroles

tags.name: Incident Notes
tags.description: This object represents the incident notes of an incident.

tags.name: Incident Tags
tags.description: This object represents the incident tags of an incident.

tags.name: Incident Responders
tags.description: This object represents the incident responders of an incident.

tags.name: Events
tags.description: This object represents the events of an integration.

tags.name: Incident Alerts
tags.description: This object represents the incident alerts of an incident.

tags.name: Tags
tags.description: This object represents the tags of an incident. You can checkout the tag docs here https://docs.zenduty.com/docs/incidenttags

tags.name: Task Templates
tags.description: This object represents the task templates of the team. You can checkout the task template docs here https://docs.zenduty.com/docs/tasktemplates

tags.name: Priorities
tags.description: This object represents the priority of an incident. You can checkout the priority docs here https://docs.zenduty.com/docs/incidentpriority

tags.name: SLA
tags.description: This object represents the SLA of an incident. You can checkout the SLA docs here https://docs.zenduty.com/docs/incidentsla

tags.name: Team Maintenance Mode
tags.description: This object represents the maintenance mode of a team. You can checkout the team maintenance mode docs here https://docs.zenduty.com/docs/maintenancewindows

tags.name: Postmortem
tags.description: This object represents the postmortem of an incident. You can checkout the postmortem docs here https://docs.zenduty.com/docs/incidentpostmortem

tags.name: OnCall
tags.description: This object represents the user who is oncall.

tags.name: Global Router
tags.description: This object represents the Global Router of the Account. You can checkout the global alert routing docs here https://docs.zenduty.com/docs/globalalertrouting

tags.name: Global Router Rules
tags.description: The Rules of the Global Router in an Account are represented by this object. Visit this link to view the documentation for the global alert routing rules https://docs.zenduty.com/docs/globalalertrouting

tags.name: Alert Rules
tags.description: Alert Rules in Zenduty are advanced conditions for incoming alerts. They dictate how Zenduty handles alerts and their incidents upon receipt, altering behavior and routing based on predefined rules and actions set by the user within Zenduty Integrations.Visit this link to view the documentation for the alert rules https://www.zenduty.com/docs/alertrules/

tags.name: Analytics
tags.description: These endpoints provide incident analytics for your account. All analytics endpoints accept POST requests with optional filter parameters and return metrics such as incident counts, MTTA, and MTTR.

roles.title: Incident Commander
roles.description: The incident commander is the person responsible for all aspects of the incident response, including quickly developing incident objectives, managing all incident operations, application of resources as well as responsibility for all persons involved. The incident commander sets priorities and defines the organization of the incident response teams and the overall incident action plan.

AnalyticsRequestBody.description: Common request body for all analytics endpoints. All fields are optional.

team_ids.description: List of team unique_id UUIDs. Empty array means all accessible teams.

user_ids.description: List of usernames. Use "Unassigned" to filter unassigned incidents.

priority_ids.description: List of team priority unique_id UUIDs.

escalation_policy_ids.description: List of escalation policy unique_id UUIDs.

tag_ids.description: List of team tag unique_id UUIDs.

child_incident.description: Set to "exclude" to exclude merged child incidents. Any other value has no effect.

tta.description: Time-to-acknowledge threshold in seconds. Requires tta_comparator.

ttr.description: Time-to-resolve threshold in seconds. Requires ttr_comparator. Must be >= tta if both are set.
